title: 烟囱别墅之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\142-烟囱别墅之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Lord Runciman informs the Marquess of Carrington that a sensitive memoir was secretly moved weeks before a man's death. The manuscripts were sent to James McGregor, a Canadian currently living in Africa, who is scheduled to arrive on the *Grangewood* this Thursday. Runciman intends to meet McGregor to request a one-month delay in the memoir's publication to allow for careful editing. The Earl of Carrington opposes inviting McGregor to the Chimney House due to a personal dislike of Canadians living in Africa.

To manage McGregor and avoid angering him, Roxane suggests employing a clever and attractive woman. After dismissing several candidates, Runciman proposes his cousin, Virginia Ruef, noting her personal charm and her familiarity with Hesslovakia due to her husband's embassy work. The Earl agrees, finding the prospect of seeing Virginia Ruef appealing.

Later, at Whitehall, George Roxton asks his assistant, Bill Everleigh, to check Virginia Ruef's telephone number at 487 Pound Street. Upon learning her phone line is out, Roxton instructs Bill to take a taxi to her home to ask if she will be available at four o'clock that afternoon for an important discussion. Bill arrives at the residence and meets Virginia, a charming twenty-seven-year-old woman. Virginia, who has lied to Bill about her phone being broken to facilitate his visit, mentions she is preparing to go to Sloane Street to purchase a new red rubber garter belt.
